Senior Data Scientist at [Fortinet](https://www.linkedin.com/company/fortinet)
...
💡 Senior Data Scientist | Data Engineering & Analytics Specialist | MSc in Data Science & Society With over 7 years of industry experience across India and Europe, I specialize in transforming data into strategic insights and scalable solutions. Currently, I serve as a Senior Data Scientist at Fortinet (since May 2022), where I work on cutting-edge analytics and machine learning initiatives that support cybersecurity solutions at scale. Before Fortinet, I spent 3 rewarding years at Exsell (DIV B.V.) in the Netherlands as a Data Engineer/Data Scientist, delivering data-driven strategies to diverse clients. My journey began in India, where I worked for 2 years as a BI Consultant and Data Engineer, bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions. 🎓 I hold a Master’s degree in Data Science & Society from Tilburg University, Netherlands, and a Bachelor’s in Computer Science, specializing in Business Analytics & Optimization, from India. My academic and professional background has shaped me into a well-rounded analytics professional with a strong foundation in both theory and practical application. 💼 Throughout my career, I’ve built a strong reputation for effective client communication, cross-functional collaboration, and delivering actionable insights from complex datasets. I take pride in crafting solutions that are not only technically sound but also aligned with business goals. 🧠 Core Skills & Expertise: Data Mining | Machine Learning & Deep Learning | Statistical Modeling ETL & Data Engineering | Data Pipelines | Big Data (Hadoop) Data Visualization | Dashboards | Business Intelligence DevOps Tools: Docker | Git | Jenkins Programming: Python (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib), R (ggplot2, Caret), SQL Visualization Tools: Tableau | Power BI | QlikView | Alteryx 🎨 Outside the office, I’m passionate about music, painting, and cooking—creative outlets that keep me balanced and inspired.

results.title: Fortinet Software Engineer Interview Questions & Guide 2026
highlights:
Fortinet Software Engineer Interview Questions
...
Fortinet Software Engineer Interview Questions & Guide 2026
...
# Fortinet Software Engineer interview questions & guide 2026
...
### What is a Software Engineer at Fortinet?
...
At **Fortinet**, a **Software Engineer** is at the heart of the company’s mission to secure the largest enterprise, service provider, and government organizations around the world. You are not just writing code; you are building the high-performance security infrastructure that powers the **Fortinet Security Fabric**. Your work directly impacts how data moves securely across global networks, requiring a deep understanding of both high-level architecture and low-level system performance.
...
This role is critical because **Fortinet** operates at the intersection of networking and security. Whether you are working on the **FortiOS** kernel, developing cloud-native security applications, or building management platforms, you will face complex challenges involving massive scale, low latency, and sophisticated threat mitigation. It is an environment where precision, architectural foresight, and a rigorous approach to software quality are essential for success.
...
### Common Interview Questions
...
The following questions reflect patterns observed in real **Fortinet** interview experiences. While the exact questions depend on your specific team and seniority, the focus remains on your ability to bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application.
...
#### Coding and Algorithms
...
Expect to demonstrate your proficiency in your primary language, often **C
...
++, Java, or
...
#### Architectural and Design Thinking
...
requires a balanced approach. You
...
, but you
...
to discuss your past
...
interviewers are
...
Key Responsibilities
...
As a **Software Engineer**, your day-to-day work centers on the development and maintenance of high-performance security software. You will spend significant time writing, testing, and debugging code, often working in close collaboration with product managers and QA engineers to ensure that features meet rigorous security standards.
...
You will also be responsible for participating in design reviews, where you will present your architectural plans and incorporate feedback from senior team members. Documenting your work is a critical, ongoing task, as is troubleshooting complex issues that arise in production environments. You are expected to be a proactive learner, keeping up with the evolving threat landscape and adopting new technologies to keep **Fortinet** solutions ahead of the curve.
...
### Role Requirements & Qualifications
...
A competitive candidate for this role typically possesses a strong foundation in computer science and a demonstrated ability to deliver production-grade software.
...
* **Must-have skills**: Proficient in at least one modern programming language (C, C++, Java, or Python), deep understanding of data structures and algorithms, and familiarity with Linux/Unix environments.
...
* **Nice-to-have skills**: Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure), knowledge of containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and prior work in network security or distributed systems.
...
A **Software Engineer** role at **Fortinet** is an opportunity to work at the cutting edge of network security, solving problems that protect global digital infrastructure. By focusing on your technical fundamentals, networking knowledge, and architectural thinking, you can significantly enhance your chances of success.

...
# Lead AI/ML Data Scientist
...
Nightfall is the AI-native, unified data loss prevention and insider risk management platform that protects sensitive data across SaaS apps, GenAI tools, email, endpoint devices, and more. Hundreds of customers, spanning AI innovators to top 10 banks, trust Nightfall to detect and stop data exfiltration at scale. Nightfall enables organizations to innovate freely without the risks of losing intellectual property or exposing customer data. Our agentic platform helps security teams regain their time by putting data loss prevention on autopilot. With automatic remediation, security violations can be resolved automatically before they become incidents, and end-users can be automatically trained and coached in the moment to self-heal violations that they introduce.
...
### About the role:
...
We are looking for an exceptional technical leader to join our growing team at Nightfall. As a Lead Applied Scientist joining the AI Engineering organization, you will create ML/NLP models and Gen AI solutions that power our Data Leak Protection (DLP) and other Security products. You will lead from the front to research and apply machine modeling techniques to solve security problems, then provide guidance to ML and Backend engineers to take systems to production. You will play a critical role in shaping the long-term architecture of the AI Platform.
...
This is a hybrid role (3 day office) based out of our Palo Alto, California, office, and a perfect opportunity to pursue your passion on Data Science and ML engineering.
...
- Creatively apply statistical and machine learning modeling techniques for solving open ended problems in the enterprise security and data leak detection space, including researching the most accurate and cost effective solutions.
...
- Create, train and fine-tune NLP, large language and other models for use in large scale, real-time environments
...
- Work with the team to take model prototypes to production through the entire ML lifecycle phases: system architecture, data creation, training, inferencing and evaluation
...
- Help shape the architecture of the AI Models and Platform to support machine learning at scale and with optimal operational cost.
...
- Minimum 5+ years of hands-on, technical development experience
- 5-10 years of experience mentoring and technically leading Data Science or ML Engineering teams.
...
- Strong expertise in Python with an additional language such as Go, C++, Java or Rust. Knowledge of data structures and algorithms is a must.
...
- Grounded in Data Science and Machine Learning algorithms with demonstrated business impact (preferably in security or related domain) through application of statistical and machine learning techniques.
...
- Proficient in training custom transformer, large language models or other deep learning techniques to solve complex NLP problems, and designing the ML systems to host models in production.
...
- Prior experience using AI in Security: Detection of Health, Named Entity, PII data, modeling security risk, etc.
- ML training and inferencing at scale with hands-on experience with horizontal and vertical scaling, including use of GPUs.
...
- Experience with Conversational and Agentic systems is a Plus.
- Experience with ML frameworks such as Sagemaker, MLflow, Vertex AI, etc.
...
- Familiarity with AWS and managed infrastructure
